agent_idea: Replace `mm512_mod_add`/`mm512_mod_sub` (which use `vpminud` on port 0) in the AVX512 packed field Add/Sub with mask-based conditionals using `vpcmpge_epu32_mask`/`vpcmplt_epu32_mask` (port 5), freeing port 0 for multiplication instructions in the butterfly inner loops and improving ILP throughput.

Replace `mm512_mod_add`/`mm512_mod_sub` (which use `vpminud` on port 0) in the AVX512 packed field `Add`/`Sub` impls with mask-based conditionals using `vpcmpge_epu32_mask`/`vpcmplt_epu32_mask` (port 5), freeing port 0 for Montgomery multiplication instructions in the butterfly inner loops to improve throughput.

Replace `vpminud`-based modular Add/Sub (port 0 only) with mask-based `vpcmpge_epu32_mask`/`vpcmplt_epu32_mask` (port 5) + masked `vpsubd`/`vpaddd` (port 0/1/5) in the AVX512 packed field, freeing port 0 pressure from the butterfly hot loops where Montgomery multiplications already saturate port 0.

Eliminate the `scale_applied` boolean flag from the inner loop of `second_half`'s scaled path by splitting the loop into an explicit first-layer (scaled) call followed by a simple for-loop for remaining layers, removing the per-iteration branch predicate from the hot path and reducing register pressure.

For layer=0 of `first_half_general` and `first_half_general_oop` (coset DFT), all `h/2` blocks share the same twiddle factor (since half_block_size=1 causes every block to clone the iterator from index 0), so pre-broadcast it once into `F::Packing` before the block loop, eliminating `h/2 - 1` redundant `vpbroadcastd` instructions per coset DFT call.

Hoist the single twiddle broadcast for `layer=0` in `first_half_general` and `first_half_general_oop` out of the per-block loop by introducing `dit_layer_uniform` / `dit_layer_oop_uniform` functions — at `layer=0`, all `h/2` blocks share the same twiddle factor (`twiddles[layer_rev][0]`), so broadcasting once before the block loop eliminates ~2^19 redundant `vpbroadcastd` instructions per coset DFT call (4 calls per `coset_lde_batch` = ~2M broadcasts saved).

Replace 2 of the 6 `vpmuludq` instructions in the AVX512 Montgomery multiplication (specifically the intermediate `q = prod * MU mod 2^32` step) with `vpmulld` instructions, which can dispatch to port 0 OR port 1 (unlike `vpmuludq` which is port 0 only), reducing port 0 pressure by 33% and improving throughput in the butterfly-dominated hot path.
